hints.Ioseph: 'Ioseph' is a proper noun — the subject of the sentence
hints.Galilaea: 'Galilaea' is a proper noun — the name of the region Galilee

hints.to send: 'to send' is 'mittere' — 'Ascendit' comes from 'ascendere' (to climb up)
hints.to set on fire: 'to set on fire' is 'accendere' — 'Ascendit' comes from 'ascendere' (ad + scandere)

hints.Present: Present 'ascendit' looks identical but context (narrative) confirms perfect tense here
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'ascendebat' — 'Ascendit' is 3rd singular perfect
hints.Future: Future would be 'ascendet' — 'Ascendit' is 3rd singular perfect

hints.accendo, accendere, accendi, accensum: These are the parts of 'accendere' (to kindle/set fire)
hints.scando, scandere, scandi, scansum: These are the parts of 'scandere' (to climb) — 'ascendere' adds the prefix ad-
hints.descendo, descendere, descendi, descensu: These are the parts of 'descendere' (to go down) — the opposite direction

hints.Nominative: Nominative 'Galilaea' looks the same, but 'a' (from) governs the ablative here
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be used for direct address — here 'Galilaea' follows the preposition 'a'
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'Galilaeae' — 'Galilaea' is ablative after 'a'

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'Galilaeae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Galilaeae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Galilaeae' ends in -ae

hints.Ascendit: 'Ascendit' is the verb itself, not the subject
hints.et: 'et' is a conjunction (and/also) — it cannot be the subject
hints.Galilaea: 'Galilaea' is in the ablative after 'a' — it names the place departed from

translation: And Joseph went up from Galilee
